scope: custom: combat -- unified resolution pipeline (integration capstone), Build-15
note: Unified combat resolution pipeline: composes every validated module through ONE path with the ratified ground-up damage -- sigma-leverage (R1+m1: pool from History, net+degree) <- weapon axes (head/hands/reach/speed -> sigma) <- armour (material/coverage + sigma-tempo penalty) -> ground-up DAMAGE (Impact x Coupling x Quality) -> wound model; grappling wired as the innermost state. INTEGRATION FINDING (the value of this pass): each module was validated in isolation with its own ad-hoc wrapper; composing them with REAL damage surfaced that the earlier duel 'finesse-race' (rapier#1, mace bottom) was an ARTIFACT of abstract hit-counting -- under real ground-up damage the mace's Impact (heft) dominated the unarmoured duel (rapier only 16-23%). FIX (bottom-up improvement): sigma-leverage now gates hit QUALITY (degree), not just who-lands -- a finesse/skill edge lands Overwhelming (vital placement) far more (LEVERAGE_TO_DEGREE=3.5, integration-tuned), so the rapier's modest Impact kills while the mace's big Impact is blunted by poor placement in skilled company; yet battlefield (armour) + low-skill mace stay dominant. Self-test 6/6: pipeline composes; leverage->hits+degree+damage unification (skilled sabre 98%); skilled duel rapier-v-mace 52%; battlefield inversion blunt 99% > cut 0% vs plate; armour mitigation 100%; grappling wired. No new mechanics besides the leverage->degree gating. Reuses R1/m1/weapon_axes_v2/armour_axes/damage_model/grappling. PROPOSAL -- Jordan-vetoable.

entries.value: R1 + m1 + weapon_axes_v2 + armour_axes + damage_model + grappling
entries.quoted_text: Integration only -- composes the validated combat modules through one resolve_exchange / run_engagement with the ratified ground-up damage. No mechanics redefined.
Reused from the ratified weapon/armour channels (W2 speed->tempo, R9 reach-control, ST1 Strength bind, m3 handling curve) -- not new here.

sigma-leverage gates hit QUALITY (degree), not just frequency: a finesse/skill edge lands Overwhelming (vital placement) more -> the whole combat advantage flows into damage. Tuned so the skilled duel favours finesse while battlefield/low-skill blunt stays dominant. The bottom-up improvement the integration surfaced.
